hypothesis: Applying NN-BAX to systems governed by density functional theory rather than the Lennard-Jones potential will reveal a crossover regime in which the computational savings from reduced DFT evaluations outweigh the NN-BAX overhead at a predictable threshold determined by the ratio of DFT wall-clock time to model retraining time, such that systems requiring more than approximately 30 seconds per DFT single-point calculation will achieve net wall-clock speedups exceeding one order of magnitude while maintaining energy barrier accuracy within 5% of the classical NEB reference.
